description:
Your goal is to organise a tiny “starter-project” folder and capture an exact snapshot of the two Python packages that the project depends on.  
    Work only inside /home/user/projects/simple_app.

    Desired final state (everything here is **mandatory** and will be automatically checked):

    1. Directory layout  
       • /home/user/projects/simple_app/  
       • /home/user/projects/simple_app/.venv/        ← a Python virtual-environment directory (directory must exist; tester only checks that it looks like a venv, not its internal timestamps)  
       • /home/user/projects/simple_app/logs/         ← ordinary directory you create for logs  
       • the files listed below must be in the exact paths shown.

    2. /home/user/projects/simple_app/requirements.txt  
       • File must contain exactly two lines, each terminated with a newline character (LF).  
       • Order is important – it **must** be this alphabetical order:  
            colorama==0.4.6  
            pyfiglet==0.8.post1  

    3. /home/user/projects/simple_app/logs/package_list.log  
       • Plain-text file with three lines, each ending in LF.  
       • Exact content (including capitalisation and spacing) must be:

            # Pip Package Snapshot
            colorama==0.4.6
            pyfiglet==0.8.post1

    4. The two packages above must be installed **inside the virtual environment** located at /home/user/projects/simple_app/.venv (not in the system interpreter).  
       • The tester will activate that venv and run “python -m pip show colorama” and “python -m pip show pyfiglet”; both commands must exit 0 and report the versions shown in requirements.txt.  
       • No specific version of pip/setuptools/wheel is enforced.

    5. You may accomplish everything with as few as 2-3 terminal commands, for example:  
       a) create the venv,  
       b) install from requirements.txt,  
       c) write the log file (the log file content is intentionally **not** the full `pip freeze`; it is only the two dependency lines, preceded by the single header line).

    Notes / hints (do NOT echo the literal commands here):  
       • Feel free to create the directory structure before creating files.  
       • The log directory does not exist yet; you must create it.  
       • The virtual environment must live in “.venv” (do not choose another name).  
       • When writing the package_list.log, ensure there are exactly three lines – no extra blank lines at top or bottom.  
       • All work must be done as the current, non-root user.  
       • The automated checker is strict about paths, file names, line order and line endings (LF).\n
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful execution:

    1. requirements.txt  
       Path: /home/user/projects/simple_app/requirements.txt  
       Contents (including final newline):  
            colorama==0.4.6\n
            pyfiglet==0.8.post1\n

    2. package_list.log  
       Path: /home/user/projects/simple_app/logs/package_list.log  
       Exact contents (including final newline after each line):  
            # Pip Package Snapshot\n
            colorama==0.4.6\n
            pyfiglet==0.8.post1\n

    3. A valid Python venv rooted at /home/user/projects/simple_app/.venv  
       • Executable: /home/user/projects/simple_app/.venv/bin/python exists.  
       • Within that interpreter, both `pip show colorama` and `pip show pyfiglet` report:  
            Version: 0.4.6 (for colorama)  
            Version: 0.8.post1 (for pyfiglet)

    The test harness will:  
       • Verify that the two files exist and match the contents above byte-for-byte.  
       • Confirm the .venv directory exists and that inside it the two exact versions of the packages are installed.  
       • Fail the task if additional packages are missing or if the files contain extra or reordered lines.
